[[File:Starmap_tatooine.jpg|thumb|250px|The Star Map of Tatooine.]]
The '''Rakatan Ruins''' of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] comprised the remains of a set of structures built by the ancient [[Rakata/Legends|Rakata]] during the height of the [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]]. They were located within a cave in the [[Eastern Dune Sea]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]], within which was contained a [[Star Map]].

==History==
Originally constructed during the height of the Infinite Empire by the Rakata and their [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]], they were rediscovered by two [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]], [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Darth Malak|Malak]], who came there for the Star Map in order to track down the [[Star Forge]]. Years later, a [[Canyon krayt dragon/Legends|canyon krayt dragon]] made its home in the ruins, and [[Bastila Shan's father]], searching for [[Krayt dragon pearl/Legends|krayt dragon pearls]] was killed by the [[Dune Sea krayt dragon|dragon]] sometime during the [[Jedi Civil War]]. At the time of his death, he carried with him a [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] with images of his daughter, [[Bastila Shan|Bastila]], as a child before she joined the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and himself.

[[Komad Fortuna]], a [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]], would later track down the beast and kill it with the assistance of the mindwiped and [[Jedi training/Legends|retrained]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Revan. With the bull dragon out of the way, the party was free to acquire data from the Star Map, this time for the Order, as well as Bastila's father's holocron, which was taken back to her mother [[Helena Shan]] in [[Anchorhead/Legends|Anchorhead]].

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' {{1st}}
*''[[Star Wars: Galaxy of Heroes]]''
